eapol_handshake_analysis.pattern: 4-way handshake progressed to Msg3 (association likely succeeded)
eapol_handshake_analysis.note: Handshake reached Msg3 — passphrase appears correct. Disconnection may be due to GTK renewal or other reason.

connection_failures.message: 44 connection failure event(s): 42× Unspecified disconnection, 1× AP inactivity timeout, 1× Unauthenticated STA sent Class 2 / 3 frame

remediations.AP inactivity timeout: AP received no frames from the STA within the configured idle timer. Recovery: reduce AP idle-timeout threshold or enable null-data keep-alives on client. Check for power-save modes suppressing uplink traffic.
remediations.Unauthenticated STA sent Class 2 / 3 fra: Frame received without a valid authentication or association context. Recovery: client must restart authentication from scratch (802.11 open auth → assoc → 4-way). Check for stale driver state after suspend/resume.
remediations.Unspecified disconnection: Check AP and client syslogs/debug logs for context around disconnect time. Recovery: reconnect; enable verbose Wi-Fi logging to capture next occurrence.

24:6a:0e:83:41:20.reason: Disassociated – AP inactivity timeout (no frames received)

c2:25:2f:7c:0c:13.reason: Class 2 frame received from non-authenticated STA

failure_details.root_cause: AP inactivity timeout — client silent for 0s
failure_details.remediation: Reduce AP idle-timeout or enable null-data keepalives on client. Disable deep power-save if EAPOL was concurrent.
failure_details.reason: Disassociated – AP inactivity timeout (no frames received)
evidence: Last data frame: 0.3s before deauth/disassoc

EAPOL frames were in progress during idle period — check whether power-save mode suppressed data frames

issues: WPA3-SAE + 4-way EAPOL handshake completed successfully (Msg1–Msg4 all seen, 5 SAE Confirm(s) with status=0).
wpa3_sae_failures.message: WPA3-SAE: 1 advisory notice(s) — WPA3-SAE + 4-way EAPOL handshake completed successfully (Msg1–Msg4 all seen, 5 S...

high_retry_rate.message: High retry rate: 59.3% of data frames are retries (threshold: 15%)

issues.description: 20 BTM Request(s) from AP(s) directing client(s) to roam. High BTM rate suggests aggressive steering — possibly due to AP overload, poor RF conditions, or band-steering policy.
issues.impact: Frequent roaming disrupts client connections; real-time applications (VoIP, video) may experience drops.
issues.remediation: Review AP controller roaming/steering policy thresholds (RSSI, load). Check for RF coverage gaps causing ping-pong roaming. If band-steering: verify 5 GHz coverage is adequate before forcing clients off 2.4 GHz.

issues.description: 11 BTM Request(s) were not accepted by clients. Clients are refusing to roam to the suggested target AP.
issues.remediation: Check if target AP is reachable with acceptable signal. Verify client supports 802.11v. Consider BTM with disassociation-imminent flag for mandatory steering.

action_frame_issues.message: 1201 action frame(s) across 3 categories: 2 issue(s) found

issues.description: 134 frame(s) with NAV > 5ms (max: 32614 μs). Excessively large Duration/NAV values can starve other stations from accessing the medium (virtual carrier sense abuse).
issues.impact: Other stations defer transmission for the NAV duration, causing unfair medium access and throughput starvation.
issues.remediation: Identify source of excessive NAV values (check TA field). Update driver/firmware. If intentional: possible NAV attack — enable AP-side protection.

control_frame_issues.message: Control frame analysis: 1 issue(s) — 14670 RTS, 11708 CTS, 0 PS-Poll, 201 BAR

issues.description: Null/QoS-Null frames are 58.6% of all data-type frames (42330 null vs 29956 real data). Excessive null frames indicate clients are in power-save most of the time, spending more time on PS signaling than data transfer.
issues.impact: Network overhead from PS management; indicates idle or poorly-configured clients wasting airtime.
issues.remediation: Review client power-save configuration. Adjust DTIM interval on AP (longer DTIM = fewer wakeups). For active clients: disable aggressive power-save in driver.

issues.description: Client 24:6a:0e:83:41:20 had 21563 power-save mode transitions across 65806 frames (32.8% of frames are PS boundary changes). Rapid PS transitions cause latency spikes.
issues.impact: Each PS transition adds latency (50-200ms for buffered frames). Rapid cycling wastes battery and airtime.
issues.remediation: Configure client to use WMM-PS (U-APSD) instead of legacy PS-Poll. Adjust PS wake interval in client driver. For latency-sensitive apps: disable power-save entirely.

power_save_issues.message: Power management analysis: 2 issue(s) — 42330 null frame(s), 29956 data frame(s)
